name: Splitwise MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Splitwise — the popular expense splitting and group cost sharing app. Enables AI agents to query group expenses, check balances, add new expenses, settle debts, and analyze spending patterns with friends, roommates, and travel groups through the Splitwise API.
use_cases: Querying Splitwise balances and outstanding debts from personal finance assistant agents
Adding expenses to Splitwise groups from AI expense tracking workflows
Analyzing group spending patterns and trip expenses from AI financial agents
Generating expense summaries and settlement recommendations from Splitwise data
not_for: Business accounting or professional expense management (Splitwise is personal/social)
Teams using Venmo, PayPal, or other payment platforms without Splitwise
High-frequency automated expense entry (Splitwise has API rate limits)
best_when: A Splitwise user wants AI assistants to help manage shared expenses — checking who owes what, adding expenses from receipts, and analyzing group spending patterns.
avoid_when: You don't use Splitwise or manage shared expenses. Also: financial settlement recommendations should be reviewed before acting.

auth.notes: Splitwise OAuth 2.0. Requires Splitwise developer account and app registration. OAuth grants access to user's groups and expenses.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Splitwise free: unlimited groups and expenses
pricing.notes: Splitwise free tier includes API access. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Splitwise API rate limits apply — adequate for personal use

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Expense entries affect real shared financial records — verify before adding
OAuth required — Splitwise developer app registration needed before use
Expense categories and descriptions matter for accurate tracking — provide meaningful data
Community MCP implementation — verify against Splitwise API documentation for latest features
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Shared expense data is personal financial information. OAuth 2.0. HTTPS. Personal financial data — treat as sensitive.

security_score_components.security_notes: Personal financial data. OAuth 2.0. HTTPS. Expense records are sensitive.
